title: salix × pustoriae
intro: Salix × pustoriae, also known as Pustorian willow, is a hybrid plant derived from the genus Salix, a group of flowering plants commonly referred to as willows. The plant contains salicin, a compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Salix × pustoriae include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to help manage conditions such as fever, arthritis, and rheumatism. In herbal preparations, Salix × pustoriae is often used in the form of tinctures, teas, and salves, which can be applied topically to relieve pain and reduce inflammation.
